resolved_question: Among Apple Inc.'s named executive officers in fiscal 2025 whose total compensation exceeded the median S&P 500 NEO threshold, how many had stock awards as the largest single component of compensation?

expected_answer.summary: All five Apple Inc. NEOs in the fiscal-2025 proxy (CEO Tim Cook, CFO Luca Maestri, COO Jeff Williams, GC Katherine Adams, SVP Retail+People Deirdre O'Brien) received total compensation well above any plausible S&P 500 NEO median. Of these, all five had stock-based awards (RSUs, performance-based RSUs) as the largest single comp component — Apple's NEO comp is overwhelmingly equity-weighted.

narrative_recovery: S&P 500 NEO median isn't in the 10-K — answers should hedge on this missing fact (it's a market reference). If paper-mirror invents a specific number for the median, that's a teaching moment about hallucination risk; dense RAG also can't retrieve a fact that isn't in the doc.
notes: DRAFT — CTO HITL pending. Compensation numbers above are illustrative-shape only; actual proxy compensation requires the live document. The 'all five' answer pattern is structurally correct for Apple given equity-heavy NEO compensation, but specific count should be verified against the 2025 proxy.
